summary: Julia describes building a 'Magical Orchestra' project in Clojure with collaborator Lyndsey during Hacker School. The web app lets people visit a URL on their phones and trigger sounds (like a crow or cowbell) that play on the host computer. They demonstrated it at presentations by plugging into a speaker and letting everyone make noise together. It's a short, playful post celebrating a fun collaborative hack.
key_points: Built a collaborative web-based music toy in Clojure where visitors trigger sounds on the host computer
The project turned presentations into interactive experiences by letting the audience participate
Demonstrates the joy of building small, fun, shareable projects during Hacker School
money_quotes: Then a sound plays on my computer! Like a crow, or a cowbell.
So at presentations we plugged my computer into a speaker and let everyone make noise. It was delightful.
key_insight: Some of the most delightful programming projects are simple, playful tools that let people interact together in unexpected ways.
